title: Bio - hCANH 3D
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2tqja","text":"Purpose                                                                                                                                                                       hCANH 3D heteronuclear dipolar correlation 1H detection experiment at fast spinning rate. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4mbds","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2aka8","text":"Scope                                                                                                                                                                          Intra-residue (CAi, Ni, Hi) backbone chemical shift correlations of CA, N and H atoms. 3D hCANH is used for sequential assignment of 15N, 13C, and 1H chemical shifts in conjugation with 3D hCONH and 3D hCA(CO)NH experiments.        ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f5u0g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4urqf","text":"Checklist for setting hCANH3d experiment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":40}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4rdhh","text":"Load parameters file or previous standard experiment acquired using the same spectrometer, probe, and spinning rate (if exist).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ujkq","text":"Updated the optimized parameters\n(p14, plw14, p13, plw13, p15, plw15, p43, spnam41, cnst41, spnam31, cnst31, p35, spnam32, cnst32, spnam52, cnst52, p54, spnam53, cnst53, spnam43, cnst43, p38, spnam38, cnst38, cpdprg4, p24, and cnst24, cpdprg3, p23, cnst23, cpdprg5, p25, cnst25, cpdprg6, p26, cnst26, and d30).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e9vgi","text":"Set carrier frequencies for each channel (O1p, O2p, O3p).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2r4a3","text":"Set acquisition time (aq), dwell time (dw), spectral width (sw), and complex points (TD) for each dimension.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d4ru3","text":"Set recycle delay (d1)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"csg3g","text":"Set NUS schedule if applicable","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13ot7","text":"Set scan numbers (NS) guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7htt1","text":"This SOP is written based on the pulse sequence developed at NMRFAM. If using other pulse sequences, the strategy is still applicable, but the parameter names will be different. Please refer to the schematic pulse sequence under \"Materials\" to find corresponding parameter names if using other pulse sequences. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ahbj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3o15m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ac851","text":"MAS rate: ~40 kHz for 1.6 mm rotor or ~60 kHz for 1.2 mm rotor. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"alqsn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1tvrb","text":"Temperature:  As determined for optimal sample sensitivity and resolution. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ff3ch","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"996en","text":"Below optimizations are required before setting up the hCANH3d experiment (parameters needs to be updated are shown in parenthesis). Example SOP for each optimization is in References.  Note that RF powers in this pulse sequence defined using kHz rather than Watt (i.e., input kHz number for rf powers and the code will calculate the corresponding Watt numbers for Topspin to use). Only the hard pulses for calibration need to be input as Watt (plw13, plw14, and plw15). "],["CP","Cross Polarization"],["MAS","Magic Angle Spinning"],["NUS","Non-Uniform Sampling"]],"guid":"E8F908987EA442428F57F550CBCA61C8","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["hCANH","CP based HNCA 3D correlation experiment
"],["CP","Cross Polarization"],["MAS","Magic Angle Spinning"],["NUS","Non-Uniform Sampling"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23]}},"1":{"type":"image","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"height":210,"id":529150,"is_video":false,"legend":"{"blocks":null,"entityMap":null}","mime":"image/png","originalFile":{},"original_name":"image.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/sez9cd5q7.png","source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/sez8cd5q7.png","type":1,"width":550}}}}
steps: Load “hCNH3d-NAN” pulse program and parameter set “hCANH3d-NAN_par”.
Use Topspin command “edc” to open a new experiment.

Optimize the pulse sequence parameters. The optimization needs to be done are listed in the "Before start" section under "Guidelines & Warnings". Example SOPs for the required optimization are attached at "References". Note that the parameters can be optimized use different methods/pulse sequences. Please consult to your facility manager to perform the optimization.
Set the 1 H carrier frequency at ~5ppm (for the best water suppression performance). Set the 13 C carrier frequency at ~55 ppm, and 15 N carrier frequency at ~ 118 ppm.
Note that the parameters will be off if using different spectrometers/probes. Please consult to your facility manager/staff to get proper starting parameter set.

Direct 1 H dimension (t 3 or F3) Default acquisition time: ~50 ms. Note that the acquisition time depends on the 1 H T 2 . However, the low power decoupling allows us use longer acquisition time. So, we recommend acquiring ~50 ms if the 1 H T 2 is unknown. The FID can be truncated using data processing software (Topspin or NMRPipe) readily Default spectral width: ~30 ppm.

Indirect 13 C dimension (t 1 or F1) Increment of delay (IN_F, s): n * t r .For example, 182 μs can be used for 38.462 kHz MAS on 600 MHz. For a given MAS rate, adjust the dwell time to integer multiples of t r that covers at least 20 ppm 13 C spectral width. Spectral width (SW, ppm): coupled with dwell time, >20 ppm is required to cover the full spectrum. Carrier frequency (O2p, ppm): 55 ppm. Hypercomplex scheme (FnMODE): States-TPPI Maximum evolution time (AQ, s): depending on 13 CA T 2 , the default is ~10 ms. The evolution time equals to (Increment of delay) * (TD). Once Increment of delay is fixed as described above, change number of TD to adjust the total evolution time.

Indirect 15 N dimension (t 2 or F2) Dwell time (IN_F, s): n * t r . For example, 468 μs can be used for 38.462 kHz MAS on 600 MHz. For a given MAS rate, adjust the dwell time to integer multiples of t r that covers at least 40 ppm 15 N spectral width. Spectral width (SW, ppm): coupled with dwell time, >40 ppm is required to cover the full spectrum. Carrier frequency (O3p, ppm): 118 ppm. Hypercomplex scheme (FnMODE): States-TPPI Maximum evolution time (AQ, s): depending on 15 N T 2 , the default is ~10 ms. The evolution time equals to (Increment of delay) * (TD). Once increment of delay is fixed as described above, change number of TD to adjust the total evolution time.
Set NUS schedule. This step is optional, but 25% NUS is recommended for 3D experiments
Adjust measurement time as required by increasing number of scans in multiples of 16.
Start the experiment and monitor the first ~20-30 rows.
Process first dimension FT to check for adequate signal correctly arraying indirect dimension.

Use recycle delay of 1.3*T 1 for maximum sensitivity per unit time. If 1 H T 1 is not measured, use the default value which is 2 s. However, measuring 1 H T 1 before setting this experiment is highly recommended for best practice.
creator: NMRFAM Facility
